name: Al-Furqaan Foundation
mission: To inform and educate all the residents of the United States the truth about Islam and its core principals and to counter the negative media information...
theoryOfChange: Al-Furqaan Foundation operates with a clear mission to deliver the message of The Quran to every individual in America, aiming to fulfill what they identify as the '5 rights of The Quran' upon Muslims: to Believe It, Recite It, Understand It, Act Upon It, and Convey It. This mission serves as their core strategic framework, guiding their various initiatives. Their work is structured across three primary verticals: Dawah Projects, Educational Projects, and Service Projects, each designed to make the Quran's message accessible. Examples of these projects include The Furqaan Project (Quran distribution), The Clear Islam (street dawah), Islam in Prison, Dawah in Spanish, Dawah in Braille, The New Muslim Foundation, and Furqaan Academy (educational institutions). The organization's vision for the future includes expanding dawah outreach globally and strengthening educational institutions to nurture confident, faithful Muslim leaders, and ensuring new Muslims find a supportive environment.
scoreSummary: AL-FURQAAN FOUNDATION shows good alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance (significant risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: Al-Furqaan Foundation explicitly states, "Donate your zakat today." Their donation pages also include an option to select "Zakat/Sadaqa" when choosing the number of Quran boxes to sponsor and as a general donation designation. Masjid Furqaan, a division of Al-Furqaan Foundation, also has a dedicated "Zakat Distribution" section on its website. (Source: https://furqaan.org/giving/)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 25 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

dimension_explanations.alignment: As a Zakat-eligible institution, the charity provides a medium fit for Muslim donors seeking to support religious and cultural dawah efforts [2]. The organization focuses on distributing 'The Clear Islam' and other products to fulfill its mission of informing the U.S. public about Islamic truths [5][6].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation demonstrates strong financial health with a total revenue of $3,249,437 and a program expense ratio of 89.3% [1][3]. While it holds 25.1 months of working capital, the institutional nature of its work results in a higher cost per beneficiary of approximately $6,846 [4][5].

baseline_narrative.headline: Al-Furqaan Foundation is a Chicago-based dawah organization dedicated to distributing the Quran and educating the American public on the core principles of Islam.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 89.3%, indicating most funds go directly to dawah and education
Exceptional fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.01 to raise every $1
Strong liquidity with 25.1 months of working capital available
baseline_narrative.summary: The Al-Furqaan Foundation operates several key programs including the Furqaan Project and Furqaan Dawah Center to counter negative media narratives through education [5]. The organization is recognized as Zakat-eligible, allowing donors to fulfill their religious obligations through various giving methods [2][6].
